title: This NEW Hermes Update Builds Agents From Scratch
summary: Hermes introduces a Blank Slate mode that lets users build AI agents from scratch by starting with minimal components and adding only necessary tools. This approach reduces bloat, prevents unwanted feature drift from updates, and simplifies agent configuration for lean use cases.
writeup: The video covers Hermes and its new Blank Slate mode, which builds AI agents from scratch by starting with minimal components and adding only the tools a given task requires. The approach targets a common problem: agents that accumulate bloat and drift in behavior as updates introduce unwanted features. The concrete takeaways are that starting lean keeps configuration simple, limits feature drift from updates, and suits narrow use cases where only specific tools are needed.
